data.title: [brainstorm/preplanning]: operational atomicity check — detect artifact-reference desync patterns in AC generation
data.description:
## Origin
Placeholder epic converted from bug 0551-3244 (feature request, not defect).

## Problem
Brainstorm and preplanning evaluate features at the capability level but do not surface operational coordination requirements — specifically, the pattern 'Script A creates/updates artifact X, but configuration file Y referencing X is not updated in the same atomic operation.' This gap produced a desync in epic 36a7-dbb5 (plugin release channels) where release.sh created tags + bumped plugin.json but did not update marketplace.json's source.ref in the same run.

## Analogous patterns
- Migration script adding a table without updating a schema registry
- Deployment script bumping a version without updating a manifest referencing it
- Installer adding a plugin entry without updating a required index file

## Proposed scope (not yet brainstormed)
- Operational atomicity check pattern added to brainstorm scrutiny pipeline or preplanning AC generation
- Red-team reviewer (dso:red-team-reviewer) is a candidate insertion point
- For any story touching a script that creates/modifies a versioned artifact, surface the question: 'Is there another file referencing this artifact that must be updated in the same operation?'
- If yes, require explicit AC: '<file> is updated atomically in the same script run/commit as <artifact>.'

## Status
Placeholder — requires /dso:brainstorm session before implementation. Tagged scrutiny:pending.
